title: Song analysis Thanatos Komm Susser Tod
display_title: Song analysis Thanatos Komm Susser Tod
wikitext:
[]: Song analysis (Thanatos, Komm Susser Tod)             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Mar 08, 2007 7:00 pm Ive been getting interested in the lyrics of these two song, but on an Non-Evangelion basis. Ill share my thoughts later when i have time. What are your guy's opinions and thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Thu Mar 08, 2007 9:56 pm Ive only heard the former, ill have to look up the other one and tell you then

[]:             Quote Post by Houndour » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:07 pm Thanatos is supposedly some figure in Greek mythology related to death. Coincidentally (prolly not), Thanatos is also a Freudian concept, referring to one's 'death drive'. This might be a reference to Kyoko's wanting to die, or thinking of it as wikipedia wrote: "an urge inherent in all organic life to restore an earlier state of things"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:11 pm Random Observation: The BGM "Thanatos" is associated with Rei, but, when Masayuki made a music video for the vocal version, he chose Misato as the subject matter.

[]:             Quote Post by C4 » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:27 pm It's really hard for me to separate these two songs, great as they are, from the Evangelion universe, just because I've always strongly associated them with certain characters. To be more specific, it's easy to see Komm, Susser Tod to be from Shinji's point of view, and I always took Thanatos ~If I Can't Be Yours~ to be Asuka's view of her relationship with Shinji. The songs to me have so much more of an emotional impact to me when placed in these contexts. Reichu wrote: Random Observation: The BGM "Thanatos" is associated with Rei, but, when Masayuki made a music video for the vocal version, he chose Misato as the subject matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:33 pm C4 wrote: I'm assuming that you got that music video from that Evangelion music video DVD? If you did, is it worth getting? To be clearer, does it have any new animation at all, or is it just clips from the series set to the music?(In which case, it would basically be a bunch of AMVs, and would thus be lame to me.)

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Fri Mar 09, 2007 1:03 am I must agree that music video was well done, I always go back and watch it time and again.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 09, 2007 1:07 am Fearthebait wrote: I must agree that music video was well done, I always go back and watch it time and again.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Fri Mar 09, 2007 4:41 am Houndour wrote: Thanatos is supposedly some figure in Greek mythology related to death. Coincidentally (prolly not), Thanatos is also a Freudian concept, referring to one's 'death drive'. This might be a reference to Kyoko's wanting to die, or thinking of it as wikipedia wrote: "an urge inherent in all organic life to restore an earlier state of things"

[]:             Quote Post by REI IKARI » Thu Mar 29, 2007 7:45 am OK. Never heard Thanatos, but I've seen Komm Susser Todd (KST) and I saw it on GUNDAM AS WELL!!! I was like, "what? Kira Yamato has stole Shinji's song!!" And I was laughing till I fell off the chair I was sitting on. It's really sad. But I thought it was from Rei's view, which makes it even more sad for me as I really like ShinjixRei.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Thu Mar 29, 2007 11:18 am Rei IKARI wrote: OK. Never heard Thanatos, but I've seen Komm Susser Todd (KST) and I saw it on GUNDAM AS WELL!!! I was like, "what? Kira Yamato has stole Shinji's song!!" And I was laughing till I fell off the chair I was sitting on. It's really sad. But I thought it was from Rei's view, which makes it even more sad for me as I really like ShinjixRei.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Fri Mar 30, 2007 4:15 pm i have i full analysis, ill type it up if anyone really wants to see it

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Fri Mar 30, 2007 11:52 pm go for it!
